# openFetch — LLM / agent context

> @hamdymohamedak/openfetch — fetch-only HTTP client (ESM). Node 18+. No axios/XHR.

## Project

- GitHub (source): https://github.com/openfetch-js/OpenFetch
- npm (package): https://www.npmjs.com/package/@hamdymohamedak/openfetch
- Docs site (GitHub Pages): https://openfetch-js.github.io/openfetch-docs/ — VitePress `base` is `/openfetch-docs/` (prefix path segments below with that base when resolving URLs).
- Path map (relative to site root): English at `/` (default), Arabic at `/ar/`, hub at `/languages/`.
- Package source layout & request lifecycle: `openFetch/docs/PROJECT_FLOW.md` (sibling monorepo path).

## Install & import

```bash
npm install @hamdymohamedak/openfetch
```

```ts
import openFetch, { createClient, createRetryMiddleware, OpenFetchError, isOpenFetchError } from "@hamdymohamedak/openfetch";
```

Default export = `createClient()` with no initial defaults.

## Client API (concise)

- `createClient(initialDefaults?)` / alias `create` — returns `OpenFetchClient`.
- Methods (all merge `defaults` + per-call config):
  - `get(url, config?)`, `head`, `options` — optional `params` for query string.
  - `post(url, data?, config?)`, `put`, `patch` — optional `data`; plain objects → JSON + `content-type: application/json` if not set; `FormData` / `URLSearchParams` / `Blob` / binary views skip JSON auto.
  - `delete(url, config?)` — use `config.data` or `config.body` if API needs a body.
  - `request(urlOrConfig, config?)` — full control (`method`, `responseType`, etc.).
- `client.use(middleware)` — pushes onto `defaults.middlewares`.
- `client.interceptors.request.use` / `response.use` — LIFO request, FIFO response.
- Throws if merged config has no `url` (`openfetch: \`url\` is required`).

## Response shape

- Default: `Promise<OpenFetchResponse<T>>` → `{ data, status, statusText, headers, config }`.
- `unwrapResponse: true` (defaults or per request): `Promise<T>` (body only).

## Config highlights

- `baseURL`, `url`, `params`, `headers`, `timeout`, `signal`, `data` / `body`, `auth` (Basic), `withCredentials`, `responseType` (`json`|`text`|`blob`|`arraybuffer`|`stream`), `validateStatus` (default 2xx), `transformRequest` / `transformResponse` (arrays concatenated), `middlewares`, `retry`, `memoryCache`, plus `RequestInit` passthrough fields.

## Errors

- `OpenFetchError` with `code`: `ERR_BAD_RESPONSE`, `ERR_NETWORK`, `ERR_PARSE`, `ERR_CANCELED`.
- Use `error.toShape({ includeResponseData: false, includeResponseHeaders: false })` before logging to clients.

## Middleware factories

- `createRetryMiddleware()` — exponential backoff; by default does not retry non-idempotent methods for network/status retries unless `retryNonIdempotentMethods: true`.
- `createCacheMiddleware(store, options)` — GET/HEAD in-memory; use `varyHeaderNames` or custom `key` for auth.

## Security

- `assertSafeHttpUrl(url)` — optional guard for untrusted URLs (http/https only; blocks literal private/loopback IPs; not a full SSRF solution).

## Framework usage

- React: `useEffect` + `openFetch.get(fullUrl)`; JSON body is `res.data`; optional `active`/`cancelled` flag on unmount.
- Vue 3: `onMounted` + `openFetch.get(fullUrl)`; set a `ref` from `res.data`.
- RSC: `await openFetch.get(fullUrl)` in an async server component.
- Beginner examples use JSONPlaceholder (no `.env`): `/framework-guides` (English), `/ar/framework-guides` (Arabic).

## Doc map (English paths)

- `/` — overview
- `/getting-started` — install, default client, middleware hook
- `/http-methods` — GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/`request` syntax
- `/framework-guides` — simple React & Vue examples (default export, `res.data`), optional RSC
- `/configuration` — `OpenFetchConfig`, merge rules
- `/interceptors-middleware` — lifecycle, ordering
- `/retry-cache` — retry + cache
- `/errors-security` — errors, logging, `assertSafeHttpUrl`
- `/languages/` — all translations

## Machine-oriented companion

For Cursor / agent skills and copy-paste prompts, see `skills.md` in this documentation repository root (excluded from VitePress build).
